title: Badge Component
content:
## Badge Component The `Badge` component is a reusable UI element designed to display small pieces of information, such as labels, statuses, or tags. It utilizes `class-variance-authority` for flexible styling based on variants. ### Key Exports * **`Badge`**: The main React component for rendering a badge. * **`badgeVariants`**: A `cva` function used internally by the `Badge` component to define its styling variants. This can also be used externally if you need to apply badge-like styling to other elements. ### Props This component accepts standard HTML `div` attributes, along with the following variant props: * **`variant`** (`'default' | 'secondary' | 'destructive' | 'outline'`): Controls the visual style of the badge. Defaults to `'default'`. * `default`: The primary badge style. * `secondary`: A secondary badge style. * `destructive`: A badge style typically used for indicating errors or critical information. * `outline`: A badge style with only an outline. * **`className`**: Allows for additional custom CSS classes to be applied to the badge. ### Usage Example ```tsx import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge"; function MyComponent() { return (
Default Badge Secondary Badge Destructive Badge Outline Badge Custom Class
); } ```
